audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Jerusalem entry, Zechariah citation, Hosanna, temple action, children praise, fig tree sign, authority dispute, two sons parable, vineyard tenants, cornerstone citation, and kingdom fruit warning.

verses.restored: When they drew near to Jerusalem and came to Bethphage, toward the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples,
saying to them, Go into the village opposite you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her. Loose them and bring them to me.
If anyone says anything to you, you shall say, The Lord has need of them, and immediately he will send them.
This happened so that what was spoken through the prophet might be fulfilled, saying:
Say to daughter Zion: Look, your king comes to you, gentle and mounted on a donkey, and on a colt, offspring of a beast of burden.
The disciples went and did as Jesus instructed them.
They brought the donkey and the colt, placed garments on them, and he sat upon them.
The very great crowd spread their garments on the way; others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the way.
The crowds going before him and following cried out, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is the one coming in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!
When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was shaken, saying, Who is this?
The crowds said, This is the prophet Jesus, from Nazareth of Galilee.
Jesus entered the temple and drove out all those buying and selling in the temple; he overturned the tables of the money-changers and the seats of those selling doves.
He said to them, It has been written, My house will be called a house of prayer, but you are making it a cave of robbers.
The blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he healed them.
When the chief priests and scribes saw the wonders he did and the children crying out in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David, they were indignant.
They said to him, Do you hear what these are saying? Jesus said to them, Yes. Have you never read, From the mouth of infants and nursing babies you prepared praise?
Leaving them, he went outside the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
Early in the morning, returning to the city, he was hungry.
Seeing one fig tree by the way, he came to it and found nothing on it except leaves only. He said to it, May fruit never come from you again. And the fig tree withered at once.
When the disciples saw, they marveled, saying, How did the fig tree wither at once?

Jesus answered, Amen, I say to you: if you have trust and do not doubt/divide, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, Be lifted and thrown into the sea, it will happen.
All things, whatever you ask in prayer, trusting, you will receive.
When he entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him while he was teaching and said, By what authority do you do these things, and who gave you this authority?
Jesus answered them, I also will ask you one word; if you tell me, I also will tell you by what authority I do these things.
The baptism of John, from where was it: from heaven or from humans? They reasoned among themselves, saying, If we say, From heaven, he will say to us, Why then did you not trust him?
But if we say, From humans, we fear the crowd, for all hold John as a prophet.
They answered Jesus, We do not know. He also said to them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.
What do you think? A man had two children. Coming to the first, he said, Child, go today and work in the vineyard.
He answered, I will not; but afterward he regretted it and went.
Coming to the other, he said the same. He answered, I go, lord, but did not go.
Which of the two did the will of the father? They said, The first. Jesus said to them, Amen, I say to you: tax collectors and prostitutes are going before you into the reign of the Divine.

For John came to you in the way of righteousness/justice, and you did not trust him; but tax collectors and prostitutes trusted him. You, seeing this, did not afterward regret/turn so as to trust him.
Hear another parable. There was a householder who planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, built a tower, leased it to farmers, and went away.
When the season of fruits drew near, he sent his servants to the farmers to receive his fruits.
The farmers took his servants; one they beat, another they killed, another they stoned.
Again he sent other servants, more than the first; they did the same to them.
Finally he sent his son to them, saying, They will respect my son.
But the farmers, seeing the son, said among themselves, This is the heir. Come, let us kill him and have his inheritance.
Taking him, they threw him outside the vineyard and killed him.
Therefore when the lord of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those farmers?
They said to him, He will bring those harmful ones to a harmful end and lease the vineyard to other farmers who will give him the fruits in their seasons.
Jesus said to them, Have you never read in the Scriptures: The stone the builders rejected, this became the head of the corner; from the Lord this came to be, and it is marvelous in our eyes?
Therefore I say to you: the reign of the Divine will be taken from you and given to a people producing its fruits.
The one falling on this stone will be broken; on whomever it falls, it will scatter him like chaff.
When the chief priests and Pharisees heard his parables, they knew he was speaking about them.
Though they sought to seize him, they feared the crowds, because they held him as a prophet.
